topic: HL Regional Option: History of Africa & the Middle East
subject: history

items.q: Did the Treaty of Versailles directly cause WWII?
items.correct: It contributed via German resentment and economic strain, but the 1930s Depression, Hitler's choices, and Allied appeasement were equally necessary — Versailles alone is not sufficient.
items.misconception: Versailles caused WWII directly because it was so harsh on Germany.
items.random_wrong: Versailles caused WWII because it required Germany to invade Poland.
items.why: Comparable peace terms have not always led to war. Modern historians stress contingency; treating Versailles as sufficient cause is a simple but inadequate story.

items.q: What were the long-term effects of the Black Death?
items.correct: Labour shortages strengthened peasants' bargaining power, weakened serfdom in England, contributed to the Peasants' Revolt, and reshaped religious authority.
items.misconception: The Black Death wiped out medieval society and ended the Middle Ages overnight.
items.random_wrong: The Black Death was caused by witches and was stopped by burning them.
items.why: The plague was catastrophic but medieval society continued. Its importance lies in slow structural changes (wages, labour, religion) — not in being a single 'ending' event.

items.correct: Mixed — failed Bay of Pigs and slow civil rights legislation, but Cuban Missile Crisis was handled, and Apollo and Peace Corps proved popular. He was killed before major legislative wins.
items.misconception: Kennedy was the greatest US president because everyone loved him.
items.random_wrong: Kennedy passed the Civil Rights Act before his assassination.
items.why: The Civil Rights Act passed under Johnson in 1964. Kennedy's reputation rests heavily on style and martyrdom; substantive achievements were limited by his short term.

items.q: Who led Ghana to independence in 1957?
items.why: Correct answer: Kwame Nkrumah. “Jomo Kenyatta” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Which 1962 accords ended the Algerian War?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Evian. “Geneva” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: How many African states gained independence in the Year of Africa?
items.why: Exact answer: 17 states. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
